title: Friday Sermon 18 January 2002
description:
In continuation of the subject of the Divine attribute of al-Razzāq [The Provider], I shall quote from The Holy Quran and elucidate its verses though Ahadith, the Holy Prophet stated that: Most definitely Allah is Pure and only accepts what is pure and wholesome and He has commanded the believers to eat from that which is pure and wholesome and to do good deeds.  Furthermore he said:
 
A person who travels widely, his hair dishevelled and covered with dust.  He lifts his hand  towards the sky (and thus makes the supplication) O Lord, O Lord, whereas his diet is unlawful, his drink is unlawful, and his clothes are unlawful and his nourishment is unlawful.  How can then his supplication be accepted?

(Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Majah.  'Sunan ibn Majah').

It was narrated that Thawban said that the Messenger of Allahﷺ said: Nothing extends one's lifespan but righteousness, nothing averts the Divine Decree but supplication, and nothing deprives a man of provision but the sin that he commits.

(Ibid).

One should never consider the issue of scarcity in planning one's family because it is Allah who provides for all His creation:

Kill not your offspring for fear of poverty.  It is We Who provider for them and for you.  Surely their killing is a great sin.

('The Holy Quran'.  Bani Israel [Children of Israel]: 32).

Family planning based on a fear of lack of provision is sinful.

Narrated Anas bin Malik: I heard Allah's Messengerﷺ saying ‘Whoever desires an expansion in his sustenance and age, should keep good relations with his kith and kin.’

(Muhammad ibn Ismail al-Bukhari.  'Sahih Bukhari').

This aspect is most neglected and that the Community still experiences lack of kindliness towards relations.

The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) said that:

One who is patient is granted the capacity to be patient, one who is content is made contended and one who adopts purity is granted purity and there is no greater provision from Allah than  patience.

(Ibid).

Abu Hurairah narrated that a man said: O Messenger of Allah!  I heard your supplication last night, and the part of it that reached me of it, was that you said ‘O Allah, forgive me my sin  and expand for me my abode and bless for me that which You have provided me.’  He said ‘Do you see that they leave off anything?’

(Ahmad ibn Hanbal.  'Musnad Ahmad ibn Hanbal').

When Allah provided the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings be on him) with food he would make a special prayer, as he would when he would drink milk, the prayer is: O Allah!  Bless this for us and provide us more of it because I do not have knowledge of any other food or drink that is complete nourishment as is milk.

(Ibn Majah).

Just as a sovereign has rules and no entry zones, the no entry zone of Allah is all that is forbidden.  The Promised Messiah (on whom be peace) stated that supreme happiness could only be achieved through God.  Man, by definition, is in some anguish or the other and the nearer one gets to Allah the greater peace one finds.  One is then also granted provisions. 

The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/sermon/FST20020118-UR.pdf
